questions.question: You're deploying inventory-service on Cloud Run behind a Global HTTP(S) Load Balancer with regional backends. A secret rotation is required: rotate DB_PASSWORD stored in Secret Manager by creating a new version and wiring the Cloud Run service to read the new value without downtime. Using only Google-native tools, specify exact steps to: 1) create a new Secret version; 2) update the Cloud Run service to use the new secret version via --secret-env-vars; 3) implement a 5% canary for 20 minutes and automatic rollback if latency > 1.2s or error rate > 0.5%; 4) configure Cloud Monitoring alerting policies; 5) rollback by shifting traffic back to the previous revision and reverting the secret to the old version. Include minimal IAM roles and CMEK considerations?
questions.answer: Create new secret version: `gcloud secrets versions add DB_PASSWORD --data-file=./new-password.txt --project=PROJECT_ID`. Deploy new revision with pinned secret: `gcloud run deploy inventory-service-v2 --image=gcr.io/PROJECT_ID/inventory-service:latest --region=us-central1 --secret-env-vars=DB_PASSWORD=projects/PROJECT_ID/secrets/DB_PASSWORD/versions/2 --no-traffic`. Configure 5% canary: `gcloud run services update-traffic inventory-service --region=us-central1 --to-revisions inventory-service-v1=95,inventory-service-v2=5`. Set up monitoring alerts: `gcloud monitoring policies create --notification-channels=EMAIL_CHANNEL --condition-duration=300s --condition-filter='metric.type="run.googleapis.com/container/request_latency" AND resource.label.service_name="inventory-service"' --condition-threshold-value=1.2 --condition-threshold-comparison=COMPARISON_GT --condition-trigger-count=1 --condition-aggregations-alignment-period=60s --condition-aggregations-per-series-aligner=ALIGN_PERCENTILE_95`. Create error rate alert: `gcloud monitoring policies create --notification-channels=EMAIL_CHANNEL --condition-duration=300s --condition-filter='metric.type="run.googleapis.com/container/request_count" AND resource.label.service_name="inventory-service" AND metric.label.response_code_class!="2xx"' --condition-threshold-value=0.005 --condition-threshold-comparison=COMPARISON_GT --condition-trigger-count=1 --condition-aggregations-alignment-period=60s --condition-aggregations-per-series-aligner=ALIGN_RATE`. Automated rollback script monitors for 20 minutes and executes: `gcloud run services update-traffic inventory-service --region=us-central1 --to-revisions inventory-service-v1=100`. Revert secret: `gcloud secrets versions enable PREV_VERSION --project=PROJECT_ID && gcloud secrets versions disable NEW_VERSION --project=PROJECT_ID`. Required IAM roles: `roles/secretmanager.secretVersionAdder`, `roles/secretmanager.secretVersionManager`, `roles/run.developer`, `roles/monitoring.alertPolicyEditor`. CMEK considerations: ensure Cloud Run service account has `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter` on the KMS key, and secret manager uses the same CMEK key.
questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to coordinate secret rotation with zero-downtime deployments using Google-native tooling and to automate rollback via observability.

## Key Concepts
- Secret Manager secret versions and access control
- Cloud Run secret-env-vars with version pinning
- Canary deployments with traffic splitting
- Cloud Monitoring alerting for SLO-based rollback triggers
- IAM least privilege and CMEK encryption requirements
- Automated rollback workflows

## Code Example
```bash
# 1. Create new secret version
echo "new-secure-password" > ./new-password.txt
gcloud secrets versions add DB_PASSWORD \
  --data-file=./new-password.txt \
  --project=PROJECT_ID

# Get new version number
NEW_VERSION=$(gcloud secrets versions list DB_PASSWORD \
  --project=PROJECT_ID \
  --limit=1 \
  --format='value(name)' | grep -o '[0-9]*')

# 2. Deploy new revision with pinned secret
gcloud run deploy inventory-service-v2 \
  --image=gcr.io/PROJECT_ID/inventory-service:latest \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --secret-env-vars=DB_PASSWORD=projects/PROJECT_ID/secrets/DB_PASSWORD/versions/$NEW_VERSION \
  --no-traffic

# 3. Implement 5% canary for 20 minutes
gcloud run services update-traffic inventory-service \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --to-revisions inventory-service-v1=95,inventory-service-v2=5

# Monitor and auto-rollback script
sleep 1200  # 20 minutes
# Check metrics and rollback if needed
if [[ $(check_metrics) == "fail" ]]; then
  gcloud run services update-traffic inventory-service \
    --region=us-central1 \
    --to-revisions inventory-service-v1=100
  gcloud secrets versions disable $NEW_VERSION --project=PROJECT_ID
fi

# 4. Monitoring alert policies
gcloud monitoring policies create \
  --notification-channels=projects/PROJECT_ID/notificationChannels/EMAIL_CHANNEL \
  --condition-duration=300s \
  --condition-filter='metric.type="run.googleapis.com/container/request_latency" AND resource.label.service_name="inventory-service"' \
  --condition-threshold-value=1.2 \
  --condition-threshold-comparison=COMPARISON_GT \
  --condition-trigger-count=1 \
  --condition-aggregations-alignment-period=60s \
  --condition-aggregations-per-series-aligner=ALIGN_PERCENTILE_95

# 5. Rollback commands
gcloud run services update-traffic inventory-service \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --to-revisions inventory-service-v1=100

gcloud secrets versions enable PREV_VERSION --project=PROJECT_ID
gcloud secrets versions disable $NEW_VERSION --project=PROJECT_ID

# IAM permissions
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding PROJECT_ID \
  --member="serviceAccount:PROJECT_NUMBER-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com" \
  --role="roles/secretmanager.secretVersionAdder"
```

This ensures zero-downtime secret rotation with automated rollback based on SLO violations.

Use only Google-native tools. Provide exact steps to build/push v3 to Artifact Registry, create serverless NEGs, configure a URL map with header-based routing, and implement rollback by routing all traffic to v2. Include IAM roles and Secrets usage?

Attach these NEGs to two backend services behind the Global HTTP(S) Load Balancer. Configure the URL map with header-based routing rules: X-User-Cohort=A routes 20% to v3 and 80% to v2, while X-User-Cohort=B routes 100% to v2. After 60 minutes, evaluate SLOs using Cloud Monitoring metrics for both cohorts. If p95 latency ≤ 0.9s and error rate ≤ 0.2% in both cohorts, update traffic weights to 100% v3. For rollback, modify the URL map to route all traffic to the v2 backend service. Required IAM roles: Artifact Registry Writer, Cloud Run Admin, Compute Network Admin, and Cloud Monitoring Viewer. questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design advanced traffic routing using header-based rules and serverless NEGs, enabling cohort-driven canaries without modifying application code paths. Emphasizes per-cohort monitoring, safe rollback procedures, and Google-native tooling integration.

## Key Concepts
- Header-based routing with URL maps and traffic weights
- Serverless NEGs and Cloud Run service management
- Per-cohort traffic distribution and rollback strategies
- Cloud Monitoring metrics and SLO evaluation
- IAM role management and Secret Manager integration

## Code Example
```yaml
# Example URL map configuration for header-based routing
name: cohort-based-url-map
defaultService: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/project-id/global/backendServices/v2-backend

hostRules:
- hosts: ['*']
  pathMatcher: cohort-matcher

pathMatchers:
- name: cohort-matcher
  defaultService: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/project-id/global/backendServices/v2-backend
  routeRules:
  - matchRules:
    - headers:
        X-User-Cohort: A
    priority: 1
    routeAction:
      weightedBackendServices:
      - backendService: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/project-id/global/backendServices/v3-backend
        weight: 20
      - backendService: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/project-id/global/backendServices/v2-backend
        weight: 80
  - matchRules:
    - headers:
        X-User-Cohort: B
    priority: 2
    routeAction:
      weightedBackendServices:
      - backendService: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/project-id/global/backendServices/v2-backend
        weight: 100
```
questions.diagram:
flowchart TD

  B --> C[URL map header rule]
  C --> D[Traffic weights: A=20% to v3, others to v2]
  D --> E[Monitoring per cohort]
  E --> F[Rollback by removing A rule]

## Code Example

```javascript
// Bash-like commands (illustrative)
// 1) create secret
gcloud secrets create db-password --replication-policy=automatic
// 2) add version (password supplied securely)
gcloud secrets versions add db-password --data-file=- << 'EOF'
$DB_PASSWORD
EOF
// 3) grant access to Cloud Run SA
gcloud run services add-iam-policy-binding health-check \
  --member=serviceAccount:health-check-sa@PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com \
  --role=roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor
// 4) deploy with secret
gcloud run deploy health-check --image gcr.io/PROJECT/health-check:latest \
  --region us-central1 --set-secrets DB_PASSWORD=db-password:latest
// 5) monitoring: create log metric and alert (simplified)
gcloud logging metrics create secret_retrieval_errors \
  --log-filter='resource.type="cloud_run_revision" textPayload:"Secret retrieval failed"'

```

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to configure a scheduled batch with Cloud Run Jobs using only Google-native tools. It also touches IAM, artifact storage, and rollback—common startup-day pains.

## Key Concepts
- Cloud Run Jobs, Artifact Registry, Cloud Scheduler, IAM minimal bindings, GCS integration

## Code Example
```bash
# Build/push
gcloud builds submit --tag us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/REPO/daily-agg:latest .

# Create job
gcloud run jobs create daily-agg --image us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/REPO/daily-agg:latest --region us-central1 --timeout 60m --env-vars SOURCE_BUCKET=gs://input,OUTPUT_BUCKET=gs://output

# Scheduler trigger
gcloud scheduler jobs create pubsub daily-agg-schedule --schedule '0 2 * * *' --time-zone 'UTC' --topic projects/PROJECT/topics/run-job --message-body '{"job":"daily-agg"}'

# IAM binding
gcloud run jobs add-iam-policy-binding daily-agg --region us-central1 --member=serviceAccount:cloud-scheduler-sa@PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com --role=roles/run.jobsRunner
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you ensure idempotency if the job runs twice?
- How would you monitor failures and alert on retries?

  --log-filter='resource.type="cloud_run_revision" AND resource.labels.service_name="students-api"'

# Error metric from logs
gcloud logging metrics create errors_rate \
  --description="error count" \
  --log-filter='resource.type="cloud_run_revision" AND resource.labels.service_name="students-api" AND (httpRequest.status>=400)'

# Placeholder for alert policy (define via policy.json in practice)

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests practical, beginner-friendly use of Google-native services to automate a nightly job and monitor it end-to-end.

## Key Concepts
- Cloud Run Jobs for batch tasks
- Artifact Registry as image store
- Cloud Scheduler for time-based triggers
- Cloud Logging log-based metrics for failures
- Cloud Monitoring alerting for SLO/alerting
- Least-privilege IAM roles for deployment

## Code Example
```javascript
# Build and push image
gcloud auth configure-docker us-central1-docker.pkg.dev
docker build -t daily-processor:latest .
docker tag daily-processor:latest us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/REPO/daily-processor:latest
docker push us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/REPO/daily-processor:latest

# Deploy Cloud Run Job
gcloud run jobs create daily-processor --image us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/PROJECT/REPO/daily-processor:latest --region us-central1

# Schedule nightly trigger (requires RUN API access)
gcloud scheduler jobs create daily-processor-schedule \
  --schedule "0 2 * * *" \
  --time-zone "UTC" \
  --http-method POST \
  --uri "https://run.googleapis.com/v1/projects/PROJECT/locations/us-central1/jobs/daily-processor:run" \
  --oauth-service-account-email service-account@PROJECT.iam.gserviceaccount.com

# Manual test
gcloud run jobs execute daily-processor --region us-central1
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you extend to handle transient failures and retry limits?
- How would you verify the alerting policy fires correctly without impacting production?

class DedupDoFn(beam.DoFn):

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

This question probes understanding of secure release pipelines on GCP, combining Binary Authorization attestations with Cloud Deploy canaries and health-based rollbacks. It tests knowledge of policy enforcement, image provenance, and least-privilege IAM.

## Key Concepts

- Binary Authorization and attestation
- Cloud Deploy pipelines
- Artifact Registry signing
- Health checks and monitoring
- IAM least privilege

## Code Example

```yaml
# Attestation policy placeholder
policy: |
  - match:
      image: "gcr.io/my-project/image-processor:v2"
    mustSatisfy:
      - attestedBy: "ci-signer"
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you extend this for regional deployments and cross-region rollback?
- How do you test attestation policy changes safely?

  E --> G[Rollback to v1]
